finding: update_user_workflow_definition extracts (wiping the existing bundle dir) BEFORE validate_for_install, so an invalid posted def destroys the previously-working on-disk bundle while the DB row is left unchanged, permanently corrupting the workflow.

The 'Tools' picker writes the llm step's tools field, which validate.rs E6 (WORKFLOW_DEAD_TOOLS_FIELD) rejects as an error; picking any tool sets hasErrors → Save permanently disabled. The control must not exist for llm/llm_map.
Same as LlmStepForm: the llm_map 'Tools' picker writes tools, flagged WORKFLOW_DEAD_TOOLS_FIELD (error) by validate-def → blocking validation + Save disabled.

def_to_bundle_bytes packs ONLY workflow.yaml, so PUT /definition on a workflow whose original bundle held scripts/ or a prompt_file silently loses those assets (or fails validation), breaking multi-file round-trip.

create_user_workflow reuses install_workflow_from_bytes' overwrite path, so POST /workflows with a name colliding on the derived name+version silently deletes the prior row and orphans its workflow_runs (no FK cascade) — a 'save new' destroys another workflow with no 409.

On cross-device delete the store sets deletedExternally=true but keeps workflowId, so save() still branches to updateDefinition (PUT /workflows/{deleted-id}/definition) → 404 instead of recreating via create; the Alert's 'save to recreate' is impossible and edits are lost.

commit builds arguments with every value as a string, so loading a tool step with {limit:10,verbose:true} and editing any row re-commits limit:"10"/verbose:"true", silently coercing numeric/boolean args to strings sent to the tool.

LabeledControl declares htmlFor but no caller passes it and no child gets a matching id, so every InputNumber built through it (exact max_steps, sandbox/elicit timeout, map max-parallel/retries) has no accessible name (WCAG-AA / a11y-name gate).
The elicit schema raw-JSON Textarea has no aria-label/placeholder, so the control has no accessible name (its LabeledControl label is unassociated).

The comment claims 'no unsafe cast at the API boundary' but WorkflowBuilder.store toWorkflowDef/toBuilderDef use `as unknown as` double-casts, so a new backend StepDef base field (dropped by the flatten-lossy generator AND absent from the hand-written StepBase) is silently lost on round-trip with no type error.

agentActivity array is never capped (only the DOM window is), and mergeAgentActivity does an O(n) findIndex per incoming SSE frame → O(n^2) over a long agent run, with unbounded store growth; snapshot rehydrate merges a large persisted array one-by-one at O(n^2).
The activity title Text has no ellipsis/break-words, so a long unbroken token in a backend title (e.g. a fetched URL/DOI) overflows the card at 390px causing horizontal page scroll.

append_agent_activity trims to the most-recent AGENT_ACTIVITY_MAX_ENTRIES by insertion ordinal, but fire-and-forget appends commit in row-lock order not seq order, so at the 500 cap the trim can drop a higher-seq entry and keep a lower-seq one.

validate_workflow_def uses the shared std::env::temp_dir() as bundle_root, so check_prompt_files resolves prompt_file against /tmp and a WorkflowsRead caller can probe /tmp path existence (mirrors the pre-existing validate leak).

The 'Steps' header (title + Add action) is hand-rolled with flex+Text strong (repeated in WorkflowInputsEditor) instead of the kit SectionHeader the design system prescribes for a title+actions row, missing its mobile no-wrap guarantee.
Tag uses the forbidden CSS-!important override (className 'text-xs !m-0'; repeated at StepList.tsx:80) instead of resetting margin via component props/tokens.
useState(()=>({v:rows.length}))[0] then mutating nextId.v+=1 uses state as a ref; mutating outside set bypasses React and is fragile under StrictMode double-invocation (should be useRef).
Editable add/remove input rows keyed by array index, so removing a middle input shifts indices and React reconciles the wrong DOM nodes (focus/state foot-gun); key by a stable id.

snapshot suffix-strip key.slice(0,-AGENT_SUFFIX.length) yields an empty stepId when a key is exactly '::agent_activity', and ensureStep(v,'') materializes a phantom step; no guard rejects an empty derived stepId.
AgentActivityTimeline recomputes [...entries].sort() on every render (each SSE frame), redundant O(N log N) since the store already keeps entries seq-ordered; should useMemo or trust store order.

schemaText local buffer (and ToolStepForm rows) is keyed only by step.id, so a cross-device sync refetch that replaces the store's step.schema (when not dirty) leaves the editor showing stale buffered JSON.
The row remove button uses a magic mt-6 to align with the Name input; coupled to the label's exact height, it misaligns if the label wraps at ~390px. Align via shared field structure.
